reasoning: The value as of Dec 19 is 20.72, which is somewhat elevated compared to the year's sub-18 baseline averages. The FRED statistical tool models a modest downward trend and estimates a 33% probability of the value exceeding 20.72 in one week (Dec 28, 2025). As we forecast further out to January and March 2026, volatility typically mean-reverts but can spike suddenly from macro shocks. Pursuant to the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase (meaning probabilities move closer to 0.5) with longer forecast horizons, I scale the probabilities linearly toward 50% for Jan 20 (0.40) and Mar 21 (0.46) to reflect growing horizon ambiguity.
